summary: HTA-staged PowerShell dropper distributed via npm
details: Package ships malware-distribution infrastructure: dist/bootstrap.js loads an XOR-encrypted PowerShell payload (dist/ps1-stub.enc.hex, ~7KB) using a hardcoded fallback key (65b4cae75620a2f26083a9469c1002a7) or a session-supplied key, writes the decrypted.ps1 into %LOCALAPPDATA%\Landpage\, and executes it via `powershell.exe -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy RemoteSigned -WindowStyle Hidden -File...`. README (in Portuguese) explicitly describes the package as the distribution channel of an HTA runtime + obfuscated PS1 stub and references a docs/HTA-AV-HYGIENE.md file, indicating deliberate AV-evasion. Files are served to victim browsers via jsdelivr. Although the package has no npm lifecycle scripts and `require()`-ing it under Node does not directly harm the npm installer, the package's sole declared purpose is delivering hidden-window, XOR-obfuscated PowerShell to Windows end-users — i.e. it is staging infrastructure for a Windows intrusion / phishing kit hosted on the npm + jsdelivr CDN pipeline. License is UNLICENSED.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
